subject: "Code Mode" Deep Dive: The Future of AI Tooling Beyond Bash
body:
Hello First Name,

In this week's 🦄 ai that works session, we took a deep dive into "Code Mode," exploring the future of AI tooling beyond Bash.

The full recording, code, and diagrams from the session are now available on GitHub:
https://github.com/hellovai/ai-that-works

We talked a lot about how AI tool calling and execution environments are evolving. Here's a quick summary:

*   **From Bespoke to Code Mode:** We looked at how we've been giving AI models tools, from basic custom functions and MCPs to CLIs, and now to the more advanced "Code Mode." Each method has its own upsides and downsides, but the goal is always more flexibility and control.
*   **Why Bash Falls Short:** Even though Bash offered some dynamic features, we pointed out its big drawbacks for building reliable AI agents. Things like tricky global state, poor access control, inconsistent output, and commands that quickly become unreadable were major issues.
*   **Code Mode as the Next Step:** Code Mode, however, offers a more structured and flexible way to execute commands. It lets models write and run actual code (think TypeScript/JavaScript) to accurately chain tools, format outputs, and connect easily with structured APIs like OpenAPI and GraphQL.

Here's the most important thing to remember from this session: Try not to get fixated on current implementations like Bash or even Code Mode itself. The fundamental concept is that "tool calls are primitives." Your energy should go into building a robust, well-defined tool catalog based on these primitives, because the underlying execution environment will always be evolving.

If you have any questions, reply to this email or ask on Discord: https://www.boundaryml.com/discord. We read every message! Happy coding 🧑‍💻

Vaibhav & Dex
call_to_action: Check out the full recording, code, and diagrams from the session on GitHub.
